---
title: "Hot Spring Richness – Normality Check"
author: "Ecologist"
date: "2026-06-25"
format: html
editor: source
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
#| warning: false
```

```{r}
#| label: packages
#| message: false

# Load all required packages.
# Some packages (e.g., piecewiseSEM, DiagrammeR) are needed later for SEM.
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(performance)
library(MASS)
library(e1071)
library(car)
library(cowplot)
library(tidyverse)
library(Hmisc)
library(linkET)
library(hier.part)
library(glmm.hp)
library(broom)
library(mgcv)
library(piecewiseSEM)
library(DiagrammeR)
library(glue)
library(scales)
```

```{r}
#| label: load-data

# Import the SEM dataset
hotspring <- read.csv("D:/2025hotspringcover/Data/SEMdata.csv", header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

#| label: clean-data

# Convert to data.frame and set row names from the CODE column
hotspring <- as.data.frame(hotspring)
rownames(hotspring) <- hotspring$CODE
hotspring <- hotspring[, -1]   # remove the CODE column

# Separate zero‑richness sites (they will be analysed differently)
hotspring <- hotspring %>%
  filter(richnesstotal != 0)
hotspring_zero <- hotspring %>%
  filter(richnesstotal == 0)

# Round species pool estimates to whole numbers
hotspring$species_pool <- round(hotspring$species_pool)

#| label: rescale

# Rescale all numeric predictors to the range [0, 1]
# This puts variables on a common scale and helps convergence of models.
mydata <- hotspring |> 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scales::rescale(.x, to = c(0, 1))))

#| label: normality-tests

# Shapiro–Wilk test for normality of total richness
shapiro_richness <- shapiro.test(mydata$richnesstotal)
shapiro_richness

# Shapiro–Wilk test for normality of VGC (a predictor)
shapiro_vgc <- shapiro.test(mydata$VGC)
shapiro_vgc

```

```{r}
#| label: gam-model
# Fit a Poisson GAM to test for a non‑linear effect of soil pH on total richness
modelrich_gam <- gam(richnesstotal ~ s(pH_soil, k = 3, bs = "cs"), 
                     data = hotspring, family = poisson)
summary(modelrich_gam)

#| label: glm-model
# Fit a simple Poisson GLM: richness explained by species pool size
modelpool <- glm(richnesstotal ~ species_pool, data = hotspring, family = poisson)
summary(modelpool)
```

```{r}
# p1: Richness ~ pH (GAM smoother)
p1 <- ggplot(hotspring, aes(x = pH_soil, y = richnesstotal)) +
  geom_point(color = "#0072B2", size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "gam",
    formula = y ~ s(x),
    method.args = list(family = "poisson"),
    se = TRUE,
    color = "#E69F00"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Soil pH", y = "Total richness") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = 1.2, color = "black"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )
p1

# p2: Richness ~ Species pool (GLM)
p2 <- ggplot(hotspring, aes(x = species_pool, y = richnesstotal)) +
  geom_point(color = "#0072B2", size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "glm",
    method.args = list(family = "poisson"),
    se = TRUE,
    color = "#E69F00"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Species pool", y = "Total richness") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = 1.2, color = "black"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )
p2

# p3: Species pool vs. pH interval (simple scatter)
p3 <- ggplot(hotspring, aes(x = pH_interval, y = species_pool)) +
  geom_point(color = "#0072B2", size = 3) +
  labs(x = "pH interval", y = "Species pool size") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = 1.2, color = "black"),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )
p3

final_plot <- (p1 | p2 | p3) + 
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")
final_plot

```

```{r}
## 5. Microhabitat Drivers of Richness
#| label: fig-microhabitat
#| fig-cap: "Non-linear relationships between local species richness and microhabitat variables. (A) Soil moisture (dashed line indicates a distinct or weaker trend), (B) Soil temperature, (C) Litter cover, and (D) Biological soil crust cover."
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 8
#| echo: true

# Define a reusable plotting function using modern tidy evaluation (.data[[]])
plot_gam <- function(data, xvar, xlabel, lty = "solid") {
  ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[xvar]], y = richnesstotal)) +
    geom_point(color = "#0072B2", size = 1.5, alpha = 0.6) +
    geom_smooth(
      method = "gam",
      formula = y ~ s(x),
      method.args = list(family = "poisson"),
      se = TRUE,
      color = "#E69F00",
      linewidth = 1.2,
      linetype = lty # Elegant way to handle line types
    ) +
    labs(x = xlabel, y = "Species Richness") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "black"),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 12)
    )
}

# Generate plots for four microhabitat variables
p_soil_m    <- plot_gam(hotspring, "Soil_m", "Soil Moisture", lty = "dashed")
p_temp_soil <- plot_gam(hotspring, "Temp_soil", "Soil Temperature")
p_litter    <- plot_gam(hotspring, "LITTER", "Litter Cover")
p_crust     <- plot_gam(hotspring, "CRUST", "Biological Soil Crust")

# Combine into a 2x2 layout using patchwork
p_all <- (p_soil_m | p_temp_soil) /
         (p_litter  | p_crust) + 
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") & 
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16))

p_all
```

```{r}
#| label: gam-multiple
# Helper: fit a GAM with a single smooth term
fit_gam <- function(data, xvar) {
  formula_gam <- as.formula(paste("richnesstotal ~ s(", xvar, ")", sep = ""))
  gam(formula_gam, data = data, family = poisson)
}

# Fit GAMs for four predictors
gam_soil_m    <- fit_gam(hotspring, "Soil_m")
gam_temp      <- fit_gam(hotspring, "Temp_soil")
gam_litter    <- fit_gam(hotspring, "LITTER")
gam_crust     <- fit_gam(hotspring, "CRUST")

# Extract key statistics
extract_gam_stats <- function(gam_model) {
  s <- summary(gam_model)
  data.frame(
    edf      = s$s.table[1, "edf"],
    chi_sq   = s$s.table[1, "Chi.sq"],
    p_value  = s$s.table[1, "p-value"],
    dev_expl = s$dev.expl
  )
}

# Combine results into a single table
gam_results <- rbind(
  Soil_m    = extract_gam_stats(gam_soil_m),
  Temp_soil = extract_gam_stats(gam_temp),
  LITTER    = extract_gam_stats(gam_litter),
  CRUST     = extract_gam_stats(gam_crust)
)

gam_results
```

```{r}
#| label: full-glm
# Fit a Gaussian GLM on rescaled data (variables now in [0,1])
model <- glm(
  richnesstotal ~ pH_soil + Soil_m + Temp_soil +  
    LITTER + CRUST +
    HS.TEMP + HS.PH +
    pH_cv + Temp_cv,
  data = mydata, family = gaussian
)
summary(model)
```

```{r}
#| label: vif
# Variance Inflation Factor – checks for multicollinearity
vif(model)
```

```{r}
#| label: dispersion
# Check overdispersion (should be close to 1 for a well‑specified Gaussian model)
dispersion <- sum(residuals(model, type = "pearson")^2) / df.residual(model)
cat("Dispersion parameter:", dispersion, "\n")

#| label: hier-part
# Hierarchical partitioning – assesses independent contributions of each predictor
iv_list <- all.vars(formula(model))[-1]  # all predictors
hp_result <- hier.part(
  y = mydata$richnesstotal, 
  xcan = mydata[, iv_list], 
  family = "gaussian", gof = "Rsqu"
)

```

```{r}
#| label: forest-plot
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-cap: "Forest plot: GLM coefficient estimates with 95% confidence intervals"

# Extract coefficients (excluding intercept)
P <- summary(model)
fixed_effects <- as.data.frame(P$coefficients) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = rownames(.),
    Lower = Estimate - 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
    Upper = Estimate + 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
    Significance = case_when(
      `Pr(>|t|)` < 0.001 ~ "***",
      `Pr(>|t|)` < 0.01 ~ "**",
      `Pr(>|t|)` < 0.05 ~ "*",
      `Pr(>|t|)` < 0.1 ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ " "
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Variable != "(Intercept)") %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(Estimate))) %>%
  mutate(Variable = factor(Variable, levels = rev(Variable)))

# Draw forest plot
forest_plot <- ggplot(fixed_effects, aes(x = Estimate, y = Variable)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#0072B2") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Lower, xmax = Upper), height = 0.2, color = "#0072B2") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1), breaks = seq(-1, 1, 0.5)) +
  scale_y_discrete(
    position = "right",
    labels = c(
      "Litter cover ***",
      "pH CV **",
      "Soil pH **",
      "Biological crust *",
      "Hotspring pH",
      "Temperature CV",
      "Soil temperature",
      "Hotspring temperature",
      "Soil moisture"
    )
  ) +
  labs(title = "GLM coefficient estimates", x = "Estimate (±95% CI)", y = "") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.y.right = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14))

forest_plot
```

```{r}
#| label: bar-plot
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-cap: "Hierarchical partitioning: independent contribution (%) of each predictor"

# Use glmm.hp to get individual contributions (works on the same model)
result <- glmm.hp(model)

# Prepare data frame with percentages and ANOVA significance
data_plot <- data.frame(
  variable   = rownames(result$hierarchical.partitioning),
  percentage = result$hierarchical.partitioning[, "I.perc(%)"]
)

anova_res <- anova(model)
significance <- data.frame(
  variable = rownames(anova_res),
  p_value  = anova_res$`Pr(>F)`
) %>%
  mutate(Significance = ifelse(p_value < 0.05, "Significant", "Not Significant"))

data_plot <- left_join(data_plot, significance, by = "variable") %>%
  arrange(desc(percentage)) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = rev(variable)))

# Draw bar plot
bar_plot <- ggplot(data_plot, aes(x = variable, y = percentage, fill = Significance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7, color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(percentage, 2)), vjust = -0.3, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Significant" = "#4DBBD5FF", "Not Significant" = "#BEBEBEFF")) +
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = c(
      "Soil pH", "Litter cover", "Hotspring pH", "pH CV",
      "Biological crust", "Temperature CV", "Hotspring temperature",
      "Soil temperature", "Soil moisture"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)), limits = c(0, 30)) +
  labs(y = "Contribution (%)", x = "", fill = "Significance") +
  ggtitle("Hierarchical Partitioning") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.line.x = element_line(size = 1.2),
    axis.line.y = element_line(size = 1.2),
    legend.position = "top",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")
  )

bar_plot
```

```{r}
#| label: combined-final
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-cap: "Combined model diagnostics: forest plot (left) and hierarchical partitioning (right)"

combined_plot <- forest_plot | bar_plot +
  plot_annotation(title = "Model Analysis: richnesstotal",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))
combined_plot
```

```{r}
#| label: sem
# Define the piecewise SEM
sem_model_updated <- psem(
  glm(LITTER ~ pH_soil + Soil_m + Temp_soil, data = mydata, family = gaussian(link = "identity")),
  glm(CRUST ~ pH_soil + Soil_m + Temp_soil, data = mydata, family = gaussian(link = "identity")),
  glm(richnesstotal ~ LITTER + pH_soil + CRUST + Soil_m + pH_cv + Temp_soil, 
      data = mydata, family = gaussian(link = "identity"))
)

summary(sem_model_updated)
rsquared(sem_model_updated)
fisherC(sem_model_updated)
```

```{r}
#| label: sem-plot-ggplot
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-cap: "SEM path diagram – standardized coefficients shown on arrows"

# ---------- Extract coefficients (with column name fix) ----------
coef_raw <- coefs(sem_model_updated, standardize = "scale")
coef_table <- as.data.frame(coef_raw)
if (any(bad <- names(coef_table) %in% c("", NA))) {
  names(coef_table)[bad] <- paste0("col", which(bad))
}

get_stars <- function(p) {
  case_when(
    p < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p < 0.01  ~ "**",
    p < 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE      ~ ""
  )
}

edges <- coef_table %>%
  transmute(
    from     = Predictor,
    to       = Response,
    est      = as.numeric(Std.Estimate),
    p        = as.numeric(P.Value),
    label    = sprintf("%.2f%s", est, get_stars(p)),
    sig      = p < 0.05,
    color    = ifelse(sig, "#E69F00", "gray80"),
    linetype = ifelse(sig, "solid", "dashed"),
    size     = ifelse(sig, ifelse(p < 0.001, 1.2, 0.9), 0.5)
  )

# ---------- Node layout ----------
rank_blocks <- "
  { rank = same; pH_soil; Soil_m; Temp_soil }
  { rank = same; pH_cv }
  { rank = same; LITTER }
  { rank = same; CRUST }
  { rank = same; richnesstotal }
"

levels_raw <- str_extract_all(rank_blocks, "(?<=rank = same; ).*?(?=\\s*\\})")[[1]]
levels <- str_split(levels_raw, ";\\s*") %>% lapply(str_trim)

all_nodes <- unique(c(edges$from, edges$to))
missing_nodes <- setdiff(all_nodes, unlist(levels))
if (length(missing_nodes) > 0) levels <- c(levels, list(missing_nodes))

n_levels <- length(levels)
node_pos <- map_dfr(seq_along(levels), function(i) {
  nodes <- levels[[i]]
  y <- n_levels - i + 1
  n <- length(nodes)
  x <- seq(-(n-1)/2, (n-1)/2, length.out = n) * 2.5
  tibble(name = nodes, x = x, y = y)
})

# ---------- Edge coordinates (fixed grouping) ----------
edges_pos <- edges %>%
  left_join(node_pos, by = c("from" = "name")) %>%
  rename(from_x = x, from_y = y) %>%
  left_join(node_pos, by = c("to" = "name")) %>%
  rename(to_x = x, to_y = y) %>%
  mutate(pair = paste(pmin(from, to), pmax(from, to), sep = "|")) %>%  # build column first
  group_by(pair) %>%
  mutate(
    curve_base = ifelse(from < to, 0.15, -0.15),
    curvature  = curve_base + (row_number() - 1) * 0.12 * sign(curve_base)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    label_x = (from_x + to_x) / 2,
    label_y = (from_y + to_y) / 2,
    nudge_y = 0.15 * ifelse(from_y > to_y, 1, -1)
  )

# ---------- Plot ----------
ggplot() +
  pmap(
    list(edges_pos$from_x, edges_pos$from_y,
         edges_pos$to_x, edges_pos$to_y,
         edges_pos$color, edges_pos$linetype,
         edges_pos$size, edges_pos$curvature),
    function(x, y, xend, yend, col, lty, sz, curv) {
      geom_curve(
        aes(x = x, y = y, xend = xend, yend = yend,
            color = col, linetype = lty, size = sz),
        curvature = curv,
        arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "inches"), type = "closed"),
        show.legend = FALSE,
        lineend = "round"
      )
    }
  ) +
  geom_label(
    data = edges_pos,
    aes(x = label_x, y = label_y + nudge_y, label = label),
    size = 3.8, fill = "white", color = "black", label.size = 0,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_label(
    data = node_pos,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = name),
    fill = "lightyellow", color = "black", size = 5,
    label.size = 0.6, fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_color_identity() +
  scale_linetype_identity() +
  scale_size_identity() +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 1.2) +
  xlim(-5, 5) +
  ylim(0.5, n_levels + 0.8) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20))
```

```{r}
#| label: effects
# Extract standardized coefficients
std <- coefs(sem_model_updated, standardize = "scale")
path_direct <- std %>%
  dplyr::select(Response, Predictor, Std.Estimate)

# Helper to extract a direct path coefficient (returns 0 if path not present)
get_std <- function(pred, resp, data = path_direct) {
  val <- data %>%
    filter(Predictor == pred, Response == resp) %>%
    pull(Std.Estimate)
  if (length(val) == 0) return(0)
  return(val)
}

# Helper for indirect effect through a mediator
get_indirect <- function(pred, mediator, outcome, data = path_direct) {
  get_std(pred, mediator, data) * get_std(mediator, outcome, data)
}

# ---- Effects for Soil_m ----
indirect_soil_litter <- get_indirect("Soil_m", "LITTER", "richnesstotal")
indirect_soil_crust  <- get_indirect("Soil_m", "CRUST",  "richnesstotal")
total_indirect_soil <- indirect_soil_litter + indirect_soil_crust
direct_soil <- get_std("Soil_m", "richnesstotal")
total_soil  <- direct_soil + total_indirect_soil

cat("Soil_m:\n")
cat("  Direct   =", direct_soil, "\n")
cat("  Indirect =", total_indirect_soil, "\n")
cat("  Total    =", total_soil, "\n\n")
#| label: effects-table
effects_table <- tibble(
  Variable = c("Soil_m", "pH_soil", "Temp_soil"),
  Direct = c(
    get_std("Soil_m",   "richnesstotal"),
    get_std("pH_soil",  "richnesstotal"),
    get_std("Temp_soil","richnesstotal")
  ),
  Indirect = c(
    get_indirect("Soil_m",   "LITTER", "richnesstotal") +
      get_indirect("Soil_m", "CRUST",  "richnesstotal"),
    get_indirect("pH_soil",  "LITTER", "richnesstotal") +
      get_indirect("pH_soil","CRUST",  "richnesstotal"),
    get_indirect("Temp_soil","LITTER", "richnesstotal") +
      get_indirect("Temp_soil","CRUST","richnesstotal")
  )
) %>%
  mutate(Total = Direct + Indirect)

effects_table

#| label: effects-plot
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-cap: "Direct, indirect, and total standardized effects of the three focal predictors"
effects_long <- effects_table %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Direct, Indirect, Total),
    names_to = "EffectType",
    values_to = "EffectSize"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Variable = factor(Variable,
                           levels = c("pH_soil", "Soil_m", "Temp_soil"),
                           labels = c("Soil pH", "Soil moisture", "Soil temperature")))

ggplot(effects_long, aes(x = Variable, y = EffectSize, fill = EffectType)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title = "Standardized Effects of Soil pH, Moisture, and Temperature",
    y = "Standardized Effect Size",
    x = NULL
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.2, 0.5)) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )
```
